rationale: The citing paper repeatedly relies on Lee (2002) to define the perceptual-versus-conceptual fluency distinction and to support prior evidence that fluency affects brand evaluation and choice. This is used in the theory section to set up hypotheses and in the general discussion to position the contribution. The paper explicitly frames its contribution as adding a third fluency type, regulatory goal fluency, relative to that prior fluency framework.
relation_description: The citing paper builds on Lee (2002)'s fluency account of brand judgment by extending it from perceptual/conceptual fluency to regulatory-goal fluency in sequential advertising contexts. It preserves the core fluency logic but adds goal compatibility/conflict as a new mechanism shaping target brand evaluations.
